title: ChroPlate - ProteinA/G
author: David Frommholz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8qrhvv6

steps.instruction: Place the ChroPlate on a clean deep-well plate. Add up to 1.5 ml sample to every well. Centrifuge 2 - 10 minutes at 400 g in a swing-out rotor. It is advisable to keep the flow-through and wash fractions for later analysis, e.g. SDS-PAGE. The centrifugation time depends on the sample's volume and viscosity. Volumes larger than 1 ml and viscous samples e.g. serum usually requires more than 5 minutes centrifugation time. For optimal binding and purity, the pH of the sample should be 7.5 - 8.5 and should contain 150 - 300 mM NaCl. An easy way to achieve this is by adding 1/11 volume 0.5 M Tris, 2 M NaCl (pH 8.0) to your sample.

Empty the deep-well plate or place the ChroPlate on a clean one. Add 500 µl wash buffer to each well and centrifuge 2 minutes at 400 g in a swing-out rotor. In case of unspecific hydrophobic and/or ionic interactions include up to 1 % Tween-20 and/or up to 0.5 M NaCl in the wash buffer.
Place the ChroPlate on a clean deep-well plate. Add 300 µl elution buffer to each well and centrifuge 2 minutes at 400 g in a swing-out rotor. Repeat two more times.

Add 500 µl wash buffer to each well, place the ChroPlate on top of a deep-well plate, and centrifuge 2 minutes at 400 g in a swing-out rotor. For counterbalance of the centrifuge a dummy filter plate is included in the kit.
Repeat the previous step. For increased purity, repeat the washing step a third time.
Add one drop of neutralization solution to every well of the deep-well plate and mix gently.
It is advisable to purify by centrifugation and not by vacuum filtration because of excessive frothing.
Assemble your vacuum device according to the manufacturer's instructions. Add 500 µl wash buffer to each well, place the ChroPlate on a waste receptacle and apply a vacuum of 100 mbar for 2 minutes.

Place the ChroPlate on a clean deep-well plate. Add up to 1.5 ml sample to every well. Apply a vacuum of 100 mbar for 2 - 3 minutes. It is advisable to keep the flow-through and wash fractions for later analysis, e.g. SDS-PAGE. Strength and time of the vacuum depend on the sample's volume and viscosity. Volumes larger than 1 ml and viscous samples e.g. serum usually require more than 2 minutes. For optimal binding and purity, the pH of the sample should be 7.5 - 8.5 and should contain 150 - 300 mM NaCl. An easy way to achieve this is by adding 1/11 volume 0.5 M Tris, 2 M NaCl (pH 8.0) to your sample.

Empty the deep-well plate or place the ChroPlate on a clean one. Add 500 µl wash buffer to each well and apply a vacuum of 100 mbar for 2 minutes. In case of unspecific hydrophobic and/or ionic interactions include up to 1 % Tween-20 and/or up to 0.5 M NaCl in the wash buffer. However, frothing appears easily when using Tween in vacuum filtration procedures.
Repeat the previous step. For increased purity, repeat the washing step a third time.
Place the ChroPlate on a clean deep-well plate. Add 100 µl elution buffer to each well apply a vacuum of 100 mbar for 2 minutes. Repeat two more times.
Add one drop of neutralization solution to every well of the deep-well plate and mix gently.
